info.name: Multi-Tenant SaaS Platform API
info.description: Enterprise-grade multi-tenant SaaS backend with JWT authentication, real-time WebSocket notifications, project/task management, nested comments, analytics dashboard, CSV/PDF/Excel exports, role-based access control, subscription billing, rate limiting, and complete tenant isolation. Built with Django REST Framework.

request.description: Retrieve current organization details including name, plan, subscription status, and limits.
Update organization name, plan, or settings.
Send organization invitation to a new team member.
Get all members of current organization with their roles.
Register a new user and automatically create their organization. Email verification required before login.
Login with email and password. Returns access token (15min) and refresh token (7 days).
Get a new access token using refresh token.
Verify email address using token received in email.
Send password reset link to email (rate limited to 3 requests per hour).
Logout and blacklist refresh token.
List all projects in current organization with pagination. Supports cursor-based pagination for infinite scroll.
Create a new project. Rate limited to 50 projects per hour for basic plan.
Get detailed information about a specific project including task count, comments, and attachments.
Update project details. Only admins and project owners can update.
Soft delete a project. Admins only.
List tasks with optional filters: status, priority, assigned_to, due_date range.

body.raw:
{
  "project": {{project_id}},
  "title": "Implement JWT Authentication",
  "description": "Add JWT token authentication with refresh mechanism",
  "priority": "high",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "due_date": "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z",
  "assigned_to": null,
  "estimated_hours": 8
}

request.description: Create a new task. Rate limited to 200 tasks per hour for basic plan.
Update task status (pending, in_progress, completed). Triggers real-time WebSocket notification.
Assign task to a team member.
Bulk update multiple tasks at once.
Get task dependencies and check if task is blocked.
Get all comments for a project. Replies are nested under parent comments with infinite depth.
Create a new top-level comment on a project.
Reply to an existing comment. Creates nested reply structure.
Delete a comment. Only comment author or admin can delete.
Get real-time dashboard statistics including project counts, task completion rates, and user activity. Cached for 5 minutes.
Real-time dashboard with 7-day project trends, completion rates, and active users.
Get revenue analytics for the last 12 months. Admin only.
Team member performance metrics including tasks completed, completion rate, and average completion time.
Real-time activity feed of all actions in the organization.
Export all projects to CSV format for Excel analysis.
Export tasks to CSV. Optional filter by project.
Generate professional PDF report of all projects.
Export projects to Excel (.xlsx) format with multiple sheets.
Generate advanced report with project forecasts, user productivity, and monthly trends.
Search across projects, tasks, and comments. Rate limited to 30 searches per minute. Results cached for 60 seconds.
Get all notifications for current user with read/unread status.
Mark all unread notifications as read.
Delete a specific notification.
Get current subscription plan, limits, and status.
Upgrade or downgrade subscription plan. Options: trial, basic, pro, enterprise.
Cancel current subscription (downgrades to trial).
Get current user profile information.
Update user profile information.
Change user password.

Establish WebSocket connection for real-time notifications. Requires token as query parameter.

When connected:
1. Create a project in another terminal
2. You'll receive instant notification
3. Messages appear in the WebSocket stream
Connect to project task updates WebSocket for real-time task status changes.
System health check endpoint. Returns status of database, Redis cache, and Celery worker.
Interactive Swagger/OpenAPI documentation for all endpoints.

exec: // Check if access token exists and is about to expire
// For demo purposes, we just log the current token status
console.log("No access token found. Please run Login request first.");
